description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the python:3-bookworm Docker image for build and publishing jobs and a fedora:41 image for an integration test, with a global before_script installing the test extras via pip. In the build stage add a build-job that runs `python -m pip install -U build` then `python -m build`, and stores the resulting wheels in the `dist/` artifact directory. Also in the build stage add a generate-pypi-attestations job that needs the build-job artifacts, runs only when `$CI_COMMIT_TAG` is present, uses the same image, installs `pypi-attestations` and signs all files in `dist/*` with `python -m pypi_attestations sign dist/*`, produces `dist/` as an artifact and requests a SIGSTORE_ID_TOKEN with audience `sigstore`. In the deploy stage add a publish-job that depends on both previous jobs, runs only on tag pipelines, installs `twine` and uploads the wheels and their attestations to PyPI using `twine upload --attestations dist/*`, and requests a PYPI_ID_TOKEN with audience `pypi`. Finally, in the test stage add an integration test job that installs dbus and pip extras, then runs `dbus-run-session -- pytest`, and separate pytest jobs for Python 3.7 through 3.13 each using the matching python:x image and executing `pytest`.
